summary: Matched sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.c by implementing HSD_GObjProc_8038FAA8, the GObjProc insertion routine, and marking the object Matching in configure.py. The implementation replaces NOT_IMPLEMENTED and removed dont_inline pragmas with C control flow that scans GObj/proc lists by p_link and s_link, updates global proc-list anchors, links the new proc into next/prev/child chains, and preserves current-proc globals. The author noted the control flow is ugly but matches; decomp.dev reported one new function match, HSD_GObjProc_8038FAA8, +365 bytes, taking main/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc from 1.09% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Implemented HSD_GObjProc_8038FAA8 and cleaned variable names in HSD_GObjProc_8038FC18 to support matching.
Changed sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).

what_changed: Replaced NOT_IMPLEMENTED in HSD_GObjProc_8038FAA8 with a full C implementation for inserting a new HSD_GObjProc.
Removed the local #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop wrapper around HSD_GObjProc_8038FAA8.
HSD_GObjProc_8038FAA8 now derives proc_gobj from gproc->gobj, s_link from gproc->s_link, and p_link from proc_gobj->p_link.
The insertion logic first checks HSD_GObj_804D7844 indexed by p_link + s_link * (HSD_GObjLibInitData.p_link_max + 1).
When an anchor exists, the function scans backward from the current GObj through cur_gobj->prev and through each GObj's proc child chain until it finds a destination proc with the same s_link.
If the found destination is the current HSD_GObj_804D7844 anchor for that p_link/s_link bucket, the anchor is updated to the new gproc.
If no destination is found in previous GObjs, the function scans lower p_link buckets in HSD_GObj_804D7844 for the same s_link.
If no valid destination exists, the new gproc is inserted at the front of the global HSD_GObj_804D7840[s_link] list.
If a destination exists, the new gproc is inserted after dst_proc using next and prev links at the insert_at_dst label.

After either insertion path, the function updates gproc->next->prev when needed, links gproc into proc_gobj->proc via gproc->child, and handles the HSD_GObj_804CE3E4.b0 / HSD_GObj_804D7830 current-proc update condition.
Renamed temporaries in HSD_GObjProc_8038FC18 from temp_r7/temp_r8 to p_link/s_link and updated uses, without changing the apparent deletion/unlink behavior.
configure.py now treats sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.c as Matching.
smart_moves: Used descriptive locals p_link, s_link, proc_gobj, cur_gobj, and dst_proc to expose the data-structure intent while still preserving matching-sensitive control flow.
Kept an explicit goto label insert_at_dst and an if (true) / else structure, reflecting that the matching shape required non-idiomatic control flow.
Documented each scan and insertion phase in comments, making ugly but matching control flow easier for future readers to audit.
Preserved repeated HSD_GObj_804D7844 index expressions instead of over-abstracting them, likely helping maintain the compiler output needed for the match.
Only flipped configure.py to Matching after the decomp.dev report confirmed the unit/function match.
decomp_lessons: For matching linked-list insertion code, readability may need to yield to source structure that matches compiler control flow; explicit goto and artificial if/else shapes can be acceptable when evidence shows they match.
When reverse-engineering baselib GObjProc behavior, track both dimensions of the global proc anchor table: p_link and s_link, indexed as p_link + s_link * (p_link_max + 1).

Insertion into HSD_GObjProc lists is coupled to multiple structures: the global per-s_link list HSD_GObj_804D7840, the p_link/s_link anchor table HSD_GObj_804D7844, each GObj's proc child chain, and current-execution globals like HSD_GObj_804D7830.

For already mostly-understood functions, simple register-derived temp names should be upgraded to semantic names once the field meaning is known, as shown by temp_r7/temp_r8 becoming p_link/s_link in HSD_GObjProc_8038FC18.
Marking an object Matching in configure.py is part of the completion step and should be backed by the decomp.dev match report.
naming_conventions: Use proc_gobj for the HSD_GObj that owns the proc being inserted.
Use cur_gobj for the backward scan through GObj prev links.
Use dst_proc for the existing HSD_GObjProc selected as the insertion destination.
Use labels with action-oriented names such as insert_at_dst when a goto is required to match control flow.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub with structured C but retained matching-oriented ugly control flow.
Used goto insert_at_dst to converge multiple search-success paths into the same linked-list insertion code.
Used if (true) { front insertion } else { insert_at_dst: ... } to preserve an otherwise unnatural branch/label layout.
Removed dont_inline pragmas once HSD_GObjProc_8038FAA8 had a matching implementation.
Changed the object status from NonMatching to Matching after HSD_GObjProc_8038FAA8 reached 100.00% in decomp.dev.

review_feedback.feedback: Author noted: "The control flow for this function is pretty ugly, but hey it matches."
Reported 1 new match: main/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc HSD_GObjProc_8038FAA8, +365 bytes, 1.09% before to 100.00% after; matched code +368 bytes and linked code +1204 bytes.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename HSD_GObj_804D7844 or HSD_GObj_804D7840 to semantic global names.
Search for additional HSD_GObjProc functions in sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.c that still use raw HSD_GObj_804D78xx globals.
Compare GObjProc insertion/deletion behavior against gobjplink, gobjgxlink, and gobjobject matching patterns.
Search for other PRs using if (true) / else label tricks to preserve matching control flow.
